mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150k/month signals robust labor market tightening, which increases inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward as investors demand higher real returns. This mechanism operates continuously in expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136133k as of Aug 2013 shows continued post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-crisis labor market recovery with tightening slack
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS growth averages below 100k over any 6-month window following signal activation, or 10-year yield declines in > 55% of 20-day windows despite PAYEMS > 150k threshold being met.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins to accelerate above 2% year-over-year, real rates turn negative, compressing bond yields and pushing the 10-year Treasury yield downward as investors flee to safety and duration.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% and CPIAUCSL at 233.318 as of Sept 2013
regime_hint: zero lower bound with emerging inflation expectations
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of 60-day windows when trigger condition is met, or Rank IC between trigger indicator and yield direction falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerating above 900k annualized units signals strengthening residential investment demand and forward GDP growth expectations. This increases long-term inflation and growth expectations, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 896k as of July 2013, near threshold
regime_hint: post-crisis housing recovery with improving credit conditions
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of triggered 20-day windows, or HOUST fails to sustain above 900k for more than 2 consecutive months after signal activation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains elevated above 7.0%, signaling slack in the economy and weak demand. This reduces inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield downward as investors anticipate slower growth and lower future rates.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.9462 and UNRATE at 7.3% as of Aug 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with persistent labor market slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of 20-day windows when both trigger conditions are met, or correlation between INDPRO deceleration and yield direction falls below -0.10 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, signaling strengthening consumer demand and forward GDP growth. This increases inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward as the market reprices growth and inflation risk over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 5-day windows following RSAFS > 0.5% MoM growth, or Rank IC between RSAFS growth and yield direction falls below 0.08 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth remains above 150k/month, signaling accelerating labor market tightening. This increases wage inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward as investors demand higher compensation for inflation risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS > 150k MoM
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.3% and PAYEMS at 136133k as of Aug 2013
regime_hint: accelerating labor market tightening in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 60% of 60-day windows when both trigger conditions are met, or wage inflation (proxied by PAYEMS growth) fails to accelerate within 6 months of UNRATE decline trigger.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains anchored near zero while real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized, creating a negative real rate environment that incentivizes investors to move out the yield curve. This pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward as investors seek higher nominal returns to compensate for negative real rates.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% and GDPC1 at 15681.0B as of Apr 2013
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with accelerating real growth
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 60-day windows when trigger conditions are met, or the relationship between negative real rates and yield direction reverses (Rank IC < -0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable below 2.5% year-over-year, signaling productivity-driven growth without inflation. This increases real growth expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward as investors reprice long-term growth without inflation risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 1.0% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.9462 and CPIAUCSL at 233.318 as of July 2013
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 60% of 20-day windows when both trigger conditions are met, or INDPRO fails to sustain > 1.0% MoM growth for more than 2 consecutive months after signal activation.
